content:
- Ralph task switch completed and updated the current/done/task-history ledgers.
- Committing all task files plus product/docs changes with the green gate evidence and the WAL-removed root cause in the message body.
- If commit succeeds, the only remaining step is git push, then I stop immediately.

- Read the progress log, then scanned .ralph/current_tasks.md and .ralph/current_tasks_done.md for active candidates, priorities, and blockers.
- Found an unblocked failing bug task: .ralph/tasks/bugs/runtime-restart-replica-can-stall-before-replaying-post-restart-writes.md. No <blocked_by> tags were present in the current task summary for that bug, so bug-first rule applies immediately.
- Wrote that task path to .ralph/current_task.txt and should stop now. If resumed, re-check current_tasks/current_tasks_done in case the backlog changed.

- Read the shared progress log and .ralph/current_task.txt on startup as required.
- Current task points at .ralph/tasks/bugs/runtime-restart-replica-can-stall-before-replaying-post-restart-writes.md.
- Next I am reading the task file to see whether it is in planning, verification, or execution state before making any edits.

- inspected runtime startup planner in src/runtime/node.rs and HA steady-state dispatch in src/ha/{decide,lower,apply,process_dispatch,worker}. confirmed startup order is inspect PGDATA -> best-effort DCS probe -> select startup mode -> execute actions.
- traced preserved-PGDATA resume path through select_resume_start_intent and managed_start_intent_from_dcs. both rebuild replica primary_conninfo from DCS/member records via basebackup_source_from_member rather than trusting stale local primary_conninfo; they error if managed replica residue exists but no authoritative leader-derived source is available.
- main remaining restart-only stall risk looks like HA two-step follow path: FollowLeader only materializes config and dispatches Demote; actual restart waits for later WaitForPostgres(start_requested=true) -> PostgresEffect::Start. skip/latch logic in ha/worker plus follow_leader_is_already_current_or_pending could still leave a stopped replica waiting if pg state or managed config state makes the dispatcher think the target is already current/pending.

- Investigated git history for HA runtime restart and degraded replica stop/restart behavior.
- Identified 9c75528 as the add point for both target e2e tests, 07552d6 as the commit that introduced explicit runtime stop semantics (`abort` + wait for API down + `pg_ctl stop immediate` + preserved restart metadata), and 94fff30 as the preserved-replica rejoin fix.
- Key causal read: the preserved replica rejoin failure was latent in product behavior for preserved PGDATA after failover to a foreign leader, but earlier tests hid it by wiping the replica data dir before restart; 07552d6 changed stop semantics but still wiped data in the degraded failover recovery path, so the stop change itself did not introduce the bug.

- Explored the current bug task, the failing scenario code, the old timeline artifact, the current ultra-long nextest log, and the related preserved-rejoin task that landed just before this one.
- Important finding: the bug task description is stale relative to current code. The scenario no longer validates node-3 after failover in the same way, and the latest nextest log for the runtime-restart scenario currently ends in success.
- Important finding: the prior fix changed FollowLeader into managed-config rewrite + Demote, but not an explicit reload/restart primitive. If this task still reproduces, the likely remaining gap is that a live replica needs a real recovery re-entry toward the new leader, not just config rewrite.
- Next I am editing the task file to record a detailed execution plan that starts by re-validating whether the bug still exists on current HEAD before making product changes.

- Wrote a detailed plan into .ralph/tasks/bugs/runtime-restart-replica-can-stall-before-replaying-post-restart-writes.md and ended it with TO BE VERIFIED as instructed.
- The plan explicitly records the stale-context mismatch: current source and latest ultra-long log suggest this bug may already be fixed by the immediately previous task, so execution must begin by re-validating reproduction on current HEAD.
- Stopping now to hand off the verification pass cleanly.

- Read prior progress on startup.
- Previous handoff says current task already has a written plan ending with TO BE VERIFIED.
- Reading current_task and the task markdown now to verify state and decide whether this turn is plan review or fresh planning.

- Read the current bug task and confirmed it was in TO BE VERIFIED state.
- Performed a skeptical plan review against the current runtime-restart scenario, current passing nextest log, follow-leader dispatch code, skip gate, and HA worker latch behavior.
- Altered the plan materially before approval: stale-duplicate verification is now the default path, and any remaining implementation work must first prove a FollowLeader skip/latch bug rather than assume a missing reload/restart primitive.
- Replaced TO BE VERIFIED with NOW EXECUTE and stopped without executing, so the next pass can follow the written plan directly without more exploration.

- Read prior progress and current_task on startup.
- Prior handoff indicates the runtime-restart replica stall task already went through planning and skeptical review, likely ending at NOW EXECUTE.
- Reading the task markdown now to confirm the exact state before I do anything else.

- Read the current bug task markdown and confirmed it is already in NOW EXECUTE state.
- Following the recorded plan literally now: first action is fresh reproduction on current HEAD with the targeted ultra-long runtime-restart scenario.
- Looking up the exact existing make/nextest invocation for that scenario so I can run it directly without detouring.

- Looked up the existing test/test-long wiring in Makefile.
- Next step is resolving the exact nextest filter for the single runtime-restart HA scenario so the reproduction attempt is narrowly attributable.

- Resolved the exact nextest identifier for the bug scenario: pgtuskmaster_rust::ha_multi_node_failover e2e_multi_node_primary_runtime_restart_recovers_without_split_brain.
- Running the single ultra-long runtime-restart scenario now on current HEAD as the first execution step.

- First fresh targeted run of the runtime-restart ultra-long scenario passed on current HEAD in about 18s.
- Executing additional back-to-back reruns now to satisfy the stale-task verification requirement before touching product code.

- The targeted runtime-restart scenario passed three consecutive times on current HEAD, so no fresh reproduction exists.
- Taking the stale/already-fixed branch from the written plan and starting the full required verification gates now: make check, make test, make test-long, make lint.

- All required gates passed on current HEAD: make check, make test, make test-long, make lint.
- No fresh reproduction exists; the targeted runtime-restart scenario passed three dedicated reruns and again inside make test-long, so this bug task is closing as stale/already-fixed.
- Updating the task markdown now to record the outcome, tick the gate boxes, and set passes=true before task switch + commit + push.

- Patched the task markdown: status=completed, passes=true, acceptance checkboxes ticked, execution outcome recorded as stale/already-fixed after repeated passing runs and full green gates.
- Running .ralph/task_switch.sh now, then I will stage all changes, commit with gate evidence in the message, and push.
